title: Fix RUN --mount=type=bind,from=<stage> not triggering preservation of <stage> rootfs
body:
The lack of the preservation was probably an oversight when --mount was added for RUN.

I added a test that fails without the modification and succeeds with.

Fixes #4375

Signed-off-by: Marcus Watkins <mwatkins@mitre.org>

heads-up: this doesn't work under podman-remote. Caught by treadmill:
```
 ✗ bud preserve rootfs for --mount=type=bind,from=                          
  ...
   # podman-remote build --force-rm=false --build-arg NONCE=Thu Oct 27 11:21:21 AM MDT 2022 \
         --layers --quiet --pull=false [...]  -f Dockerfile.3 .../bud/cache-stages
   5c7c9a83b8511d81050fdf1b1eb60498133034d203abe0c6d428920c6fca98b6
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: podman-remote build [...] 
   #| expected: =~ 'Worked' 
   #|   actual:    '5c7c9a83b8511d81050fdf1b1eb60498133034d203abe0c6d428920c6fca98b6' 
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

> heads-up: this doesn't work under podman-remote. Caught by treadmill:

Hmm, so it builds, it just doesn't get the output? I've never used `podman-remote`. I added the expected as an extra sanity test, it can be removed with no real loss.

I'm curious, though, how then some of the [existing tests](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/f87ac93b1cb6337be2e860c428e02287b4ff7c65/tests/bud.bats#L223) pass?
@marwatk my apologies, this was meant for podman/buildah maintainers, not for you. This is podman's problem to solve: someone needs to do the magic wiring on the podman side. Sorry for the confusion!

> heads-up: this doesn't work under podman-remote. Caught by treadmill:

>          --layers --quiet --pull=false [...]  -f Dockerfile.3 .../bud/cache-stages

Following PR should fix this: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4387 for `podman-remote`
